what_is_supported_now: The current five-row public-sector slice supports a bounded heterogeneity claim across one official project-page row, two direct regulator-document rows, one Amsterdam register-rich but buyer-side-unresolved row, and one Helsinki register-plus-family-level same-buyer supplier-consistent award row.

paper_thesis_use: Main results paragraph, thesis methods/results bridge, reviewer-response core claim.

what_is_supported_now: Amsterdam is the richest current exact-row register exemplar because supplier and governance fields are public while buyer-side trace remains unresolved on tested public routes; Helsinki sits one rung higher on buyer-side continuity because a same-buyer supplier-consistent award package is public, while still stopping short of exact row-linked proof.
forbidden_overread: Do not caption Amsterdam as procurement-absent or contract-resolved, and do not caption Helsinki as exact contract-linked procurement proof, page-specific award proof, or public-assessment completeness.
next_authority_upgrade: A genuinely different Amsterdam official procurement/assessment surface or a page-specific Helsinki companion explicitly tied to the row.
visualization_hook: Traceability-ladder overlay; Amsterdam caution badge; Helsinki linkage badge.
